title: Responsible Redirect
summary: You gathered strong evidence, kept Raka's trust, and redirected him away from the scheme toward safer learning and verification.
feedback: Best outcome: you combined risk awareness, sharia reasoning, and careful communication.

title: Scam Prevented, Relationship Damaged
summary: You stopped the transfer, but Raka felt attacked and may hesitate to ask for help next time.
feedback: Evidence matters, but trust and tone shape whether people can act on it.

title: Too Late
summary: The deadline passed before you made a clear recommendation. Raka transferred the money under pressure.
feedback: Time pressure is part of the scam pattern. Prioritize the highest-signal evidence earlier.

title: Misled by Hype
summary: Dimas's confidence and social proof pushed the recommendation toward joining the scheme.
feedback: Guaranteed returns, urgency, and recruitment rewards should trigger caution, not excitement.

title: Partial Understanding
summary: You avoided the scheme, but the explanation was incomplete or lacked stronger sharia reasoning.
feedback: Replay to connect risk evidence with concepts like gharar, maysir, and transparent profit-loss sharing.
